steps.title: Identify what “truly” means in context
steps.content: The passage says “This is where Pheus truly shines” and then explains that performing makes him a “more heightened version of himself.” The word “truly” intensifies “shines,” so the answer must be a word that means genuinely or actually.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Obediently’: means in a compliant manner. Shining obediently makes no sense; compliance has nothing to do with excelling in performance. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Scarcely’: means barely or almost not. “Pheus scarcely shines” contradicts the passage’s clear praise of his performance. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Impolitely’: means rudely. Nothing in the passage suggests Pheus’s performance is rude or inappropriate. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Really’: means genuinely or actually. “Pheus really shines” captures the authentic intensity the passage describes when he performs. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
